choiceresponse.label:
Thomas and Stella are analyzing a model’s prediction for the same input. Thomas uses Grad-CAM and sees strong attention on the shoreline. Stella uses LIME and finds that a few specific words/features dominate the explanation. They are surprised that the explanations do not fully agree.
Which statements best reflect what this situation tells them? (Select all that apply.)

checkboxgroup.choice: Different explanation methods can emphasize different aspects of the same prediction.
If two explanation methods disagree, at least one of them must be wrong.
Agreement between multiple explanation methods can increase confidence but does not guarantee correctness.
Disagreement indicates that the model is unstable and cannot be trusted for any inputs.

div.p: Grad‑CAM and LIME are designed to answer different questions, even when applied to the same model and the same input. Grad‑CAM focuses on where the model finds visual evidence in an image, while LIME focuses on which features or tokens matter. When multiple explanation methods align, this can increase confidence in the explanation, but agreement does not imply correctness or causality. Disagreement alone does not mean the model is unreliable; it often reflects the complexity of the model and the different questions each explanation method is designed to answer.
